qa_pairs.question: Where is the Singularity executable located on the Cedar system?
qa_pairs.answer: On the Cedar system, the Singularity executable is located at `/opt/software/singularity-x.x.x`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the path to the Singularity executable on Graham?
qa_pairs.answer: The path to the Singularity executable on Graham is `/opt/software/singularity-x.x.x`.

qa_pairs.question: How do users invoke Singularity on Niagara?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can invoke Singularity on Niagara by first loading the module (`module load singularity`) and then using `/opt/singularity/2`.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two main ways to invoke a container from a CVMFS repository?
qa_pairs.answer: A container from a CVMFS repository can be invoked either directly, where the image will be cached, or by pulling the image and storing it in your local area.

qa_pairs.question: Why might pulling a CVMFS container image and storing it locally be beneficial?
qa_pairs.answer: Pulling a CVMFS container image and storing it in your local area may provide some performance benefits, depending on the system.
